title: Parley: A Privacy-Preserving Age Verification Protocol
description: Normative specification of the Parley zero knowledge age verification protocol. Defines cryptographic primitives, wire formats, and protocol flows for the issuance, proof, and verification roles. A wallet holding a date of birth credential proves it satisfies an age threshold to a verifier without revealing the date of birth, credential signature, or any other identifying information. Uses Groth16 over BLS12-381, Pedersen commitments on Jubjub, RedJubjub credential signatures, Ed25519 issuer attestations, Blake2s-256 hashing, and PKCE session binding.

creators.name: Maelstrom AI Pty Ltd
creators.affiliation: Maelstrom AI Pty Ltd ATF Maelstrom AI Holding Trust

references: Ben-Sasson, E., Chiesa, A., Tromer, E., Virza, M. Scalable Zero Knowledge via Cycles of Elliptic Curves. CRYPTO 2014.
Groth, J. On the Size of Pairing-Based Non-interactive Arguments. EUROCRYPT 2016.
Bowe, S., Chiesa, A., Green, M., Miers, I., Mishra, P., Wu, H. Zexe: Enabling Decentralized Private Computation. 2018.
RFC 7693, The BLAKE2 Cryptographic Hash and Message Authentication Code.
RFC 7636, Proof Key for Code Exchange (PKCE).
